items.title: Kings County Roadtrip, PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>There’s never been a better time to hit the road and support Canadian businesses and communities. Hop in and buckle up as we take a trip through Kings County, Prince Edward Island! 🚗🗺️

Transcription
Let’s take a trip through Kings County, PEI!
This 2-day itinerary is jam packed with authentic sights, flavours and experiences. Follow along!
Stop #1 Overnight at 45 Steps Inn in St. Peter’s Bay
Stop #2 Spend a rejuvenating morning at Mysa Nordic Spa
Stop #3 Head to the Eastern-most point of PEI to see the East Point Lighthouse
Stop #4 Learn about the history of railroad on the Island at the Elmira Railway Museum 
Stop #5 In the friendly seaside town of Souris, walk the boardwalk and enjoy fresh seafood for dinner
Stop #6 Catch a live music show at the Souris Show Hall
Stop #7 Drive south and spend the night at Rodd Brudenell River Resort in Cardigan
Stop #8 Get out on the water with Tranquility Cove Adventures
Stop #9 Pick up a sandwich and fresh local berries at Nabuurs Gardens
Stop #10 Step back in time for an afternoon at Roma National Historic Site
Stop #11 Grab a coffee and shop around Montague
Stop #12 Check out Bogside Brewing Taproom for dinner 
Stop #13 Drink in the sweeping views at Double Hill Cidery in Caledonia
Stop #14 Wrap up your trip with a one-of-a-kind stay at Nellie’s Landing in Murray Harbour!
Thanks for coming along on our road trip through Kings County, PEI!
Got wanderlust? Hit the road and support Atlantic Canadian businesses!</article>

items.title: H20: Home to Overseas Conference, 2025 – Halifax, Nova Scotia
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Halifax is buzzing with ocean innovation! The H2O Conference is back—Canada’s biggest ocean tech event and a global hub for marine innovation. Powered by ACOA and hosted by the Ocean Technology Council of Nova Scotia, it all wraps June 12 with Cove Demo Day!
#MarineTech #OceanInnovation

Transcript
So H2O is a platform that we use to showcase our ocean technology sector.
It’s a way for our companies to come together and showcase their technologies and innovations and work with partners from around the world.

The conference has really grown and scaled over the last ten years.

It started out really as an industry showcase specifically for companies here in Nova Scotia.

But we've really grown it into an international conference with companies from all across Canada and around the world. 

Having our international partners here is very important for the H2O conference and for our ocean technology sector as a whole.

We really have worked to turn H2O into an anchor event  that's on the calendar of international partners and members of the ocean tech sector
around the world.

So for us the ocean technology sector is predominantly driven by export.

So about 80% of our company's revenues come from export.

And with that we need to have partnerships; connections with our international markets.

And the H2O conference is a way to kind of bring those partners in 
and showcase what we do best here in Atlantic Canada.</article>

items.title: Cruise Canada and New England Symposium, 2025 - Halifax, Nova Scotia
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Cruise tourism is making a big impact in Atlantic Canada. With support from ACOA, the Atlantic Canada Cruise Association is helping local communities thrive — one port at a time. Discover how Halifax and our region are welcoming the world while fueling inclusive economic growth across the East Coast.

Transcript
Halifax, Nova Scotia is hosting the Cruise Canada and New England Symposium, where cruise leaders explore new ideas, technology and opportunities.
Cruise ships help anchor Atlantic Canada's economy. 
With ACOA’s support, the Atlantic Canada Cruise Association champions our region as a top cruise destination, growing an industry that supports small businesses, boosts tourism, and enriches life in coastal communities.
And in return we offer more than a stop. We share a story told through heritage, food, music and East Coast spirit.
From our harbours to our hospitality, Atlantic Canada welcomes the world.</article>

items.title: Maritime Paper Products – Dartmouth, Nova Scotia
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Maritime Paper Products is a leading manufacturer of corrugated containers. An independent, family-owned company in Nova Scotia since 1931. With support from ACOA, the company is investing in modern, energy efficient equipment that will reduce waste and lower production expenses – reducing its carbon footprint and saving money at the same time.

Transcript
Maritime Paper Products is a leading manufacturer of corrugated containers.
 
It’s an independent, family-owned company, established in Nova Scotia in 1931.
 
Corrugated cardboard is seen as a positive environmental option in the packaging sector.
 
Most cardboard is 100% recyclable, and bio-degradable within one year.
 
With assistance from ACOA, Maritime Paper has purchased advanced equipment to reduce waste and lower production costs.
 
By using less power, and diverting more waste, Maritime Paper expects to save money and help ensure success for generations to come.</article>

items.title: Libra - Charlottetown, PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Libra is a non-alcoholic craft beer company committed to helping its customers find their balance. Since 2020, Libra has secured distribution in 1,000 retail locations and restaurants across Canada including Sobeys, Loblaws, IGA, and Costco.

Transcript
Libra is a non-alcoholic craft beer company committed to helping its customers find their balance.
While working in the beverage industry, Mitch Cobb and Mike Hogan found themselves constantly sampling products and socializing. A non-alcoholic beer seemed like the answer.
The brewers couldn't find a non-alcoholic craft beer that tasted like the real thing - so they made their own. 
After 2 years of research and development, Libra Non Alcoholic Craft Beer was launched in 2020. Libra is brewed just like a regular craft beer, with none of the alcohol and fewer calories.
Leveraging the assets and relationships of Libra’s sister company Upstreet, the brew-master has created an agile product development pipeline.
The non-alcoholic beer category is expected to grow at a rate of 7.8% over 10 years to $43.6B,  according to Future Market Insights.
Libra is recognized as a leading brand by international media and the craft beer community.
The brewery has won several international beer awards, including "Canadian Non-Alcoholic Brewery of the Year" at the New York International Beer Competition.
Since 2020, Libra has secured distribution in 1,000 retail locations and restaurants across Canada including Sobeys, Loblaws, IGA, and Costco.
As retailers continue to see an increase in demand for non-alcoholic beer, Libra is focused on becoming the category leader in North America.
The company is educating retailers and consumers on the benefits of incorporating non-alcoholic beverages to live a healthier lifestyle.
In 2021, Canadian folk artist Serena Ryder was appointed Libra’s Chief Balance Ambassador. Together, they went on a mission to normalize socializing without alcohol.
“Libra completely aligns with my core values ... it’s everything that I believe in, and I really love the beer.” - Serena Ryder
Libra is proudly B Corp Certified, a global community of companies verified to meet high social and environmental standards.
B Corps around the world are working together to redefine success in business and build a more inclusive and sustainable economy.
ACOA is proud to support Libra as they create positive change and set the standard for the non-alcoholic beverage market.</article>

items.title: Cape Smokey – Ingonish, Nova Scotia
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Cape Smokey ski hill in Cape Breton is upgrading its power systems from the mid-point to the top of the hill to meet growing energy needs, make more snow, and phase out diesel for cleaner air and less noise. 

Transcription
Cape Smokey is making more snow and more tourism opportunities with more efficient energy. New, modern power systems at the ski hill will help meet energy and snowmaking needs while replacing diesel, for cleaner air and less noise. 
Investing in clean growth pays off:  new markets, more jobs, accelerated growth, while minimizing environmental impacts.  
SMEs like Cape Smokey are leading the way by adopting new technologies and approaches to doing business.</article>

items.title: Bear River First Nation, N.S.
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content: <article>We’re grateful for projects that connect us to the earth and one another. Bear River First Nation, #Mikmaki is embracing those connections to drive economic development.</article>

items.title: Town of Salisbury’s new amphitheatre - NB
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Salisbury will soon host a variety of events, bringing people together in their new, vibrant community space. From concerts to community gatherings, the Salisbury amphitheatre is set to become a cornerstone of local entertainment and engagement. Plus, with the addition of a new trail system for walking and biking, there's even more to explore and enjoy. #gc 

Transcript:
So, we've had this dream for many, many years. But of course, it takes funding to do these types of things. 
And as a small community, we don't always have the resources to make these things happen. 
So obviously when we approached ACOA with the idea and the relevance as well as the opportunities and the benefits for our community ACOA came forward obviously with funding.
The importance of the amphitheater as well as the connecting trail to our residents is something that they've been asking for for many, many years.
As Canada's most active community in 2023, we show that having a very connected community is important where people can walk trails and do recreational activities.
The amphitheater was one of those where we could bring people together in our community, connecting place where we can grow as one. And we're just so thrilled to have this happening in our community.</article>

items.title: BioAccelerator Groundbreaking - PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Congratulations to the PEI BioAlliance on the official groundbreaking of the BioAccelerator! 🔬🧪🧬 

Learn more about the new facility that is propelling the region’s bioscience cluster forward: https://peibioalliance.com/news/government-industry-partnership-breaks-ground-on-50-million-bioaccelerator/

Transcript
BIOACCELERATOR GROUNDBREAKING

The P.E.I. BioAlliance has broken ground on the BioAccelerator, a 60,000 sq. ft. facility that will provide essential infrastructure for the region’s bioscience and life sciences industry.

Expected to be completed in 2026, the facility will be occupied by researchers, entrepreneurs, and bioscience-based companies.

PEI’s bioscience community is widely recognized for its collaborative economic cluster model that drives innovation and sector growth.

The sector now includes 60 bioscience companies earning over $600 million annually in sales, making bioscience the second-largest industry in P.E.I.

The PEI Bioscience Cluster has surpassed 2025 growth targets and is well on its way to becoming a billion-dollar industry by 2030.

ACOA is proud to support this major advancement in Prince Edward Island’s bioscience innovation ecosystem!</article>

items.title: Fall Food Tourism - PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content: <article>Fall is the perfect time to showcase the best of Prince Edward Island through food and music. The region is capitalizing on its distinct strengths and offering authentic cultural experiences to build a sustainable tourism industry that feels welcoming to everyone.</article>

items.title: Port Charlottetown - PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content: <article>Congratulations to Port Charlottetown on another busy cruise ship season, bringing in an estimated 167,000 visitors to Prince Edward Island in 2024. 🚢👋  #GC</article>

items.title: Stratford Waterfront Gathering and Event Space - PEI
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content: <article>The Town of Stratford recently announced the grand opening of the Stratford Waterfront Gathering and Event Space. This new infrastructure, supported through ACOA’s Innovative Communities Fund, is part of the community’s vision for a more inclusive, greener, and sustainable future.</article>

items.title: Magnetic Hill Zoo - Moncton, NB
items.author: ACOACanada
items.content:
<article>Ready for a wild adventure? 🦁🐾
Check out the @MagneticHillZoo, Atlantic Canada’s largest zoo! It is home to 70+ species and is sure to educate and inspire.</article>
